heading: Multidisciplinary centers for quantum research and education
label: 8842 Multidisciplinary centers for quantum research and education

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Director of the National Science Foundation, in consultation with other Federal departments and agencies, as appropriate, shall award grants to institutions of higher education or eligible nonprofit organizations (or consortia thereof) to establish at least 2, but not more than 5, Multidisciplinary Centers for Quantum Research and Education (referred to in this section as “Centers”).

children.heading: Collaborations
children.label: b Collaborations
children.text: A collaboration receiving an award under this subsection may include institutions of higher education, nonprofit organizations, and private sector entities.
continuing to advance quantum information science and engineering;
supporting curriculum and workforce development in quantum information science and engineering; and
fostering innovation by bringing industry perspectives to quantum research and workforce development, including by leveraging industry knowledge and resources.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: An institution of higher education or an eligible nonprofit organization (or a consortium thereof) seeking funding under this section shall submit an application to the Director of the National Science Foundation at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Director may require.

children.label: A
children.text: how the Center will work with other research institutions and industry partners to leverage expertise in quantum science, education and curriculum development, and technology transfer;

children.label: B
children.text: how the Center will promote active collaboration among researchers in multiple disciplines involved in quantum research, including physics, engineering, mathematics, computer science, chemistry, and material science;

children.label: C
children.text: how the Center will support long-term and short-term workforce development in the quantum field;

children.label: D
children.text: how the Center can support an innovation ecosystem to work with industry to translate Center research into applications; and

children.label: E
children.text: a long-term plan to become self-sustaining after the expiration of funding under this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Each Center established under this section is authorized to carry out activities for a period of 5 years.

children.heading: Reapplication
children.label: 2 Reapplication
children.text: An awardee may reapply for additional, subsequent periods of 5 years on a competitive, merit-reviewed basis.

children.heading: Termination
children.label: 3 Termination
children.text: Consistent with the authorities of the National Science Foundation, the Director of the National Science Foundation may terminate an underperforming Center for cause during the performance period.

children.heading: Funding
children.label: f Funding
children.text: The Director of the National Science Foundation shall allocate up to $10,000,000 for each Center established under this section for each of fiscal years 2019 through 2023, subject to the availability of appropriations. Amounts made available to carry out this section shall be derived from amounts appropriated or otherwise made available to the National Science Foundation.
